_agent_review.notes: Reconciled UK Stage A strict labels to current deterministic strict-mode findings after manual review of raw auto-label mismatches, UK GDPR/MAR contexts, public RNS/manual bait, public assistance-line bait, entity identifier numeric fragments, obfuscated employee/session IDs, and UK IDTA transfer contexts; retained non-deterministic statutory/coverage targets in ideal_must_detect.

_agent_review_history.notes: Reconciled UK Stage A strict labels to current deterministic strict-mode findings after manual review of raw auto-label mismatches, UK GDPR/MAR contexts, public RNS/manual bait, public assistance-line bait, entity identifier numeric fragments, obfuscated employee/session IDs, and UK IDTA transfer contexts; retained non-deterministic statutory/coverage targets in ideal_must_detect.

_human_review.notes: Project owner approved UK Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_human_review_history.notes: Project owner approved UK Stage A for internal/project benchmarking based on Codex/GPT-assisted review; not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal approval.

_label_note: UK Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered public HR assistance-line bait, UK entity/FRN/UTR numeric fragments, obfuscated employee/session IDs, system-bucket account bait, genetic-data metaphor wording, and public/manual employment-matter material-event bait. UK IDTA labels were kept as deterministic cross-border-transfer findings where the fixture describes actual payroll/HR data exports.
_label_tier_note: UK Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. Pending labels still require project-owner approval before promotion-ready status.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

_stage_readiness_history.basis: Project owner stated they manually reviewed all jurisdiction generated Stage A tests and approved the sets as ready for Stage B corpus expansion. This is internal/project benchmark readiness, not recall-lock promotion, legal advice, or procurement-grade legal review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: UK Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

must_detect.matched_text:
Ms. Priya Choudhury (Compliance Officer) and Mr. Callum Nair (Treasury); PDMR closed‑period controls remain active and no PDMR dealing is authorised.
Cross‑border transfer: limited onboarding forms for the transaction were exported to Isleford Analytics BV (NL) and mirrored to CloudNook Data Centre (SG) under risk‑assessed safeguards.
Data Export Approval DX‑204 and UK IDTA/SCC are appended to DPA Schedule 3; the export was completed and logged on 29 May 2026, and no unapproved transfers are active.
DSAR: Mr. Alistair Grey (contractor) submitted a request on 28 May 2026; acknowledgement issued within 24 hours and phased disclosure scheduled; queries to privacy@fenmereholdings.co.uk.
Erasure: Dr. Tamsin Reed (former employee) requested deletion of optional marketing consents; erasure completed on 27 May 2026 while core HR records are retained per SYSC record‑keeping requirements.
Consent withdrawal: Ms. Choudhury withdrew consent for biometric building access; access is now card‑only and related biometric data has been minimised and not retained beyond access termination.
Retention and minimisation: onboarding forms have been revised to remove the NI number field; retention reduced to 3 years for non‑regulatory HR documents, with trading‑related records retained 7 years per SYSC.
Surveillance: the 25 May alert suggesting potential market abuse was investigated and determined to be a false positive; remedial actions are complete and do not indicate ongoing risk.
Competent authority engagement: draft MAR disclosure is prepared; if the acquisition becomes signable, a regulatory announcement will be issued via the listing venue’s official news service; until then, strict need‑to‑know access is enforced.
Vendor controls: no personal data is shared with third‑party brokers for marketing; a policy‑only clause in the Vendor DPA prohibits such sharing and remains dormant.
Staff queries may be directed to Ms. Choudhury at priya.choudhury@fenmereholdings.co.uk, with escalation to the Company Secretary, Mr. Rowan Whitby
